query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Degeneracy', 'The state of being physically or morally deteriorated', 'Her degeneracy became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Degenerate', 'Having fallen below a desirable level, especially morally', 'What seemed degenerate at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Degradation', 'A lowering from one''s standing in office or society', 'Her degradation became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Degrade', 'To lower in value or social position', 'The author hoped the new policy would degrade tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dehydrate', 'To lose or remove water, to dry', 'The author hoped the new policy would dehydrate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deify', 'To make a god of (something or someone)', 'Over time, the community learned to deify its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deign', 'To condescend, to accept as appropriate to one''s dignity', 'Critics argued that the plan might deign the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deist', 'Someone who believes in the existence of a supreme being', 'The essay highlights the deist of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deity', 'A god or goddess, divine status', 'The essay highlights the deity of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deject', 'Make sad or dispirited', 'Her deject became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dejection', 'A state of melancholy or depression, low spirits, the blues', 'The essay highlights the dejection of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Delectable', 'Pleasing to the taste, delicious', 'Critics argued that the plan might delectable the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Delectation', 'Great pleasure, delight', 'Her delectation became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Delegate', 'To entrust (a task or responsibility)', 'The author hoped the new policy would delegate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deleterious', 'Harmful or damaging', '“Smoking has deleterious effects on health.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deliberate', 'To consider carefully', 'Critics argued that the plan might deliberate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deliberation', 'Careful consideration or discussion before making a decision', 'Her deliberation became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Delicacy', 'The quality of being delicate', 'Her delicacy became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Delicate', 'Easily broken or damaged; fragile', 'Critics argued that the plan might delicate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Delineate', 'To describe or outline precisely', '“The artist delineated the details of the portrait carefully.”', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Delineation', 'A precise description', 'The essay highlights the delineation of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deliquesce', 'To melt and disappear', 'Critics argued that the plan might deliquesce the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Delirious', 'In a state of wild excitement or ecstasy', 'The passage presents a delirious portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Delirium', 'An acutely disturbed state of mind that occurs in fever', 'Her delirium became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Delude', 'To deceive someone into believing something which is false', 'Critics argued that the plan might delude the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Deluge', 'To flood with water', 'Critics argued that the plan might deluge the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Delusion', 'A false belief that is resistant to confrontation with fact', 'The author hoped the new policy would delusion tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Demagnetize', 'To remove the magnetic propreties of something', 'Critics argued that the plan might demagnetize the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Demagogue', 'A leader who espouses the cause of the common people', 'The essay highlights the demagogue of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Demarcate', 'Set apart or distinguished from something else', '“The two regions are clearly demarcated from each other by a river.”', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Demarcation', 'The action of fixing the boundary or limits of something; a dividing line', 'Scholars noted a striking demarcation between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Demean', 'To debase, to do something that is beneath one''s dignity', 'Critics argued that the plan might demean the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Demeanor', 'A person''s conduct or behaviour', 'The essay highlights the demeanor of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dement', 'Insane or mentally ill', 'The passage presents a dement portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Demerit', 'A quality of being inadequate, a fault, a disadvantage', 'The essay highlights the demerit of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Demise', 'A person''s death', 'Her demise became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Demobilize', 'To release someone from military duty', 'The author hoped the new policy would demobilize tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Demographic', 'A specific group within a population', '“The company targeted a younger demographic with its ads.”', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Demolish', 'To destroy, to full or knock down (a building)', 'Over time, the community learned to demolish its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Demonstrable', 'Clearly apparent or capable of being logically proved', 'What seemed demonstrable at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Demonstrate', 'Clearly show the truth of (something) through proof', 'Over time, the community learned to demonstrate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Demonstrative', 'That serves to demonstrate, show or prove', 'Critics argued that the plan might demonstrative the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Demonstrator', 'A person who takes part in a public protest meeting or march', 'Her demonstrator became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Demote', 'To lower the rank or status of something', 'Critics argued that the plan might demote the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Demulcent', 'Soothing or softening', 'The passage presents a demulcent portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Demur', 'To raise doubts or objections or show reluctance', 'Critics argued that the plan might demur the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Demure', 'Reserved, modest, and shy', 'What seemed demure at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Demurrage', 'The delaying in port of a ship beyond the time allowed', 'The essay highlights the demurrage of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dendroid', '(of an organism or structure) tree-shaped, branching', 'The essay highlights the dendroid of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Dendrology', 'The study of trees and other woody plants', 'Scholars noted a striking dendrology between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
